title: RSI — Failed First Intubation Attempt
description: A full-stomach RSI in an obese adult with deterministic failure of attempt one and a scoreable oxygenating rescue before attempt two.
learningObjectives: Recognize deterministic failed placement while the airway remains unsecured
Deliver adequate mask PPV with cricoid pressure before another attempt
Secure the airway on attempt two and obtain sustained continuous EtCO2

debrief.summary: Attempt one is configured to fail after a full timed laryngoscopy. The airway remains a mask airway until adequate PPV with cricoid pressure precedes successful attempt two.
teachingPoints: A failed attempt consumes time and oxygen stores without securing the airway.
Rescue PPV must measurably restore oxygenation before the next attempt.
Attempt and desaturation timing come from the shared airway-procedure history.
